name: Persimmon Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------FILLING-----------------------------------
1 1/2 cups persimmon pulp (5-6 fruits)
1 1/2 cups cream
2 tablespoons flour
1 cup sugar
2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
4 egg yolks
1 whole egg
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
------------------------STICKY PECAN PRALINE CRUMBLE------------------------
2 cups pecan halves or pieces
1 cup sugar
1/4 cup water
1/8 teaspoon cream of tartar
For best results, persimmons must be riper than you think they are. They ripen best at room temperature, standing up—no bags, no ripening bowls. When they look rotten, shriveled, and feel like there's jelly under their skins, they're ready. This can take weeks.
Persimmon Filling:
1. Set oven rack in the center of the oven and preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Scoop persimmon pulp out of skins, discarding seeds and leaves.
3. Puree pulp in a food processor or blender.
4. Whisk together cream, flour, sugar, and melted butter.
5. Beat together the yolks, the whole egg, and the vanilla and whisk into cream mixture.
6. Fold in persimmon pulp.
7. Pour filling into pie shell.
8. Top with a handful (about 1/2 cup) of praline crumble.
9. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes.
10. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake 50 minutes to 1 hour more. Rotate the pie if the crust begins to overbrown, or cover the rim with an aluminum foil strip.
11. Filling will nearly boil when done.
Sticky Pecan Praline Crumble:
1. Toast pecans on a cookie sheet at 350°F for about 10 to 15 minutes, tossing them a few times.
2. Butter another cookie sheet and set aside.
3. In a heavy saucepan, bring sugar, water, and cream of tartar to a boil over medium-high heat.
4. If crystals form on the sides of the pan, cover it for 30 seconds or as long as 5 minutes so steam rinses off the sides.
5. When the sugar darkens, swirl the pan to even the color. The caramel should turn the color of light hay.
6. Remove from heat and stir in toasted nuts using a wooden spoon until they are coated well with caramel.
7. Spread caramel-coated nuts on the buttered cookie sheet and let cool for 1 hour.
8. Chop coarsely with a knife (a food processor causes the caramel to stick and clump).
You can store the surplus in a tightly covered jar at room temperature for three months.
